observed_problem: The project needs a transparent production-shaped baseline before adding query understanding, semantic search, LTR, personalization, or behavior signals.
decision: Use OpenSearch BM25 over title, abstract, and text as the v0 Cranfield retrieval and ranking baseline.
evidence: Worker endpoint tests validate generated OpenSearch multi_match requests and explain metadata; fixture evaluation validates metric artifact shape.
metrics_after: Fixture evaluator reports MAP 1.0, nDCG@10 1.0, Precision@10 0.8889, Recall@10 1.0, MRR 1.0 on sample data; live metrics pending.
latency_impact: API and OpenSearch took fields are exposed; live latency pending.
indexing_impact: Introduces cranfield-v0 strict OpenSearch mapping and sample bulk payload.

observed_problem: Live Cranfield failures clustered into distinct behaviors, especially late first relevant rank, zero relevant top-10 results, broad low-recall needs, lexical noise, graded ranking loss, and partial recall.
decision: Reject query-rescue because metrics regressed; keep field-sum as the best Cranfield candidate but do not promote it to the public default until BEIR transferability is measured.
metrics_before: Baseline MAP 0.2402, nDCG@10 0.2995, Precision@10 0.2316, Recall@10 0.3994, MRR 0.5350.
metrics_after: Field-sum MAP 0.2452, nDCG@10 0.3022, Precision@10 0.2418, Recall@10 0.4136, MRR 0.5499; query-rescue regressed all core metrics.
latency_impact: Not promoted to public default; live evaluation completed successfully but no public latency claim is made.
indexing_impact: No index mapping or corpus reload required.

observed_problem: Field-sum improved aggregate metrics but did not reduce zero-relevant-at-10 failures; 19 of 28 baseline zero-at-10 queries had a judged relevant document between ranks 11 and 50.
decision: Keep coverage-rerank as the best current Cranfield candidate, using field-sum top-50 retrieval plus deterministic title/abstract coverage reranking, but do not promote it to the public default until BEIR transferability is measured.
metrics_before: Baseline MAP 0.2402, nDCG@10 0.2995, Precision@10 0.2316, Recall@10 0.3994, MRR 0.5350; field-sum nDCG@10 0.3022.
metrics_after: Coverage-rerank MAP 0.2531, nDCG@10 0.3095, Precision@10 0.2502, Recall@10 0.4283, MRR 0.5520.
latency_impact: Candidate retrieves and reranks top 50 results; not promoted to public default and no public latency claim is made.
indexing_impact: No index mapping or corpus reload required.

observed_problem: Coverage-rerank improved ranking of top-50 candidates but remaining failures still showed vocabulary mismatch and late-ranking behavior.
decision: Keep prf-rerank as the best current Cranfield candidate, using field-sum top-50 retrieval plus pseudo-relevance feedback terms, but do not promote it to the public default until BEIR transferability is measured.
metrics_before: Baseline MAP 0.2402, nDCG@10 0.2995, Precision@10 0.2316, Recall@10 0.3994, MRR 0.5350; coverage-rerank nDCG@10 0.3095.
metrics_after: PRF-rerank MAP 0.2696, nDCG@10 0.3253, Precision@10 0.2676, Recall@10 0.4466, MRR 0.5620.
latency_impact: Candidate retrieves and reranks top 50 results; not promoted to public default and no public latency claim is made.
indexing_impact: No index mapping or corpus reload required.

observed_problem: The Cranfield BERT paper reports NDCG@20 values above 0.4, which are not directly comparable to the project's graded nDCG@10 ladder.
decision: Add binary relevance mode and a separate paper-style BM25 index profile for comparison, while keeping prf-rerank candidate-only and the public default unchanged.
metrics_before: Default BM25 binary NDCG@20 0.4187; default PRF binary NDCG@20 0.4542.
metrics_after: Paper-style BM25 baseline binary NDCG@20 0.4196; paper-style BM25 plus PRF binary NDCG@20 0.4547; external paper BM25 0.4714 and BERT 0.5525/0.5670.
latency_impact: No public runtime change; paper-style index is comparison-only.
indexing_impact: Created separate cranfield-v0-paper-bm25 index with BM25 k1=1.5 and b=0.75; did not replace cranfield-v0.
transferability_result: Deferred to Phase 2 BEIR and future supervised neural reranker work.

observed_problem: Prior PRF rerank was the best local Cranfield candidate, but second-stage expansion and phrase-coherence refinements still needed measured evidence.
decision: Reject prf-expand-rerank because it regressed core metrics; keep refined prf-rerank with phrase coherence weight 0.01 as the best current Cranfield candidate, but do not promote it to public default until transferability is measured.
metrics_before: Prior PRF MAP 0.2696, nDCG@10 0.3253, Precision@10 0.2676, Recall@10 0.4466, MRR 0.5620.
metrics_after: Refined PRF MAP 0.2699, nDCG@10 0.3260, Precision@10 0.2680, Recall@10 0.4469, MRR 0.5622; PRF-expanded retrieval regressed to nDCG@10 0.3219.
latency_impact: Refined PRF adds local phrase scoring only; expanded retrieval would add a second OpenSearch call and was rejected.
indexing_impact: No index mapping or corpus reload required.

observed_problem: The project needed real local embedding evidence after GEN-018 only had deterministic local-hash vector-control evidence.
decision: Keep vector/hybrid as evaluated infrastructure, but do not promote it because Ollama llama3.1:8b embeddings remained below refined PRF.
metrics_before: Refined PRF nDCG@10 0.3260 and binary nDCG@20 0.4563; field-sum cache nDCG@10 0.3022 and binary nDCG@20 0.4319.
metrics_after: Ollama vector-only nDCG@10 0.0282; best Ollama hybrid nDCG@10 0.3035; best Ollama binary nDCG@20 0.4343, all below refined PRF.
latency_impact: No public runtime change; offline local embedding generation took long enough to require checkpointing and progress reporting.
indexing_impact: No OpenSearch index mapping or corpus reload required; remote vector index creation deferred until offline evidence beats refined PRF.
transferability_result: Deferred; architecture not promoted to Phase 2 because Cranfield evidence is below current best.

observed_problem: Failure analysis and candidate-pool oracle metrics show substantial ranking headroom, and the operator asked whether LTR should be tried before neural reranking using local embeddings as features.
decision: Keep LTR as evaluated infrastructure but do not promote it because cross-validated LTR remains below refined PRF.
metrics_before: Field-sum nDCG@10 0.3022; refined PRF nDCG@10 0.3260; refined PRF binary nDCG@20 0.4563.
metrics_after: Best cross-validated LTR nDCG@10 0.3166 and binary nDCG@20 0.4529; in-sample LTR reached nDCG@10 0.3338 and binary nDCG@20 0.4607 but is capacity evidence only.
latency_impact: No public runtime change; offline LTR training and evaluation completed locally against cached retrieval pools.
indexing_impact: No OpenSearch index mapping, LTR plugin setup, or corpus reload required; remote LTR deployment deferred until cross-validated evidence beats refined PRF.
transferability_result: Deferred; architecture not promoted to Phase 2 because Cranfield cross-validation evidence is below current best.

observed_problem: Coordinate-ascent LTR had ranking signal but did not beat refined PRF, so a stronger local LTR model was needed before escalating to neural reranking or external embeddings.
decision: Keep boosted-tree LTR as evaluated infrastructure but do not promote it because cross-validated boosted-tree LTR remains below refined PRF.
metrics_before: Best coordinate LTR nDCG@10 0.3166 and binary nDCG@20 0.4529; refined PRF nDCG@10 0.3260 and binary nDCG@20 0.4563.
metrics_after: Depth-3 boosted-tree LTR reached cross-validated nDCG@10 0.3216 and binary nDCG@20 0.4457; in-sample boosted trees reached nDCG@10 0.3564 and binary nDCG@20 0.4840 but remain capacity evidence only.
latency_impact: No public runtime change; offline boosted-tree training and evaluation completed locally against cached retrieval pools.
indexing_impact: No OpenSearch index mapping, LTR plugin setup, or corpus reload required; remote LTR deployment deferred until cross-validated evidence beats refined PRF.
transferability_result: Deferred; architecture not promoted to Phase 2 because Cranfield cross-validation evidence is below current best.

observed_problem: Local-hash and Ollama llama3.1:8b embeddings did not beat refined PRF, so a dedicated retrieval embedding model was needed before judging vector/hybrid search.
decision: Keep BGE vector/hybrid as the strongest offline candidate and require remote OpenSearch vector/hybrid validation before promotion or public/default changes.
metrics_before: Refined PRF nDCG@10 0.3260 and binary nDCG@20 0.4563; best Ollama hybrid nDCG@10 0.3035 and binary nDCG@20 0.4343.
metrics_after: BGE vector-only nDCG@10 0.3419 and binary nDCG@20 0.4748; best BGE hybrid nDCG@10 0.3533 and binary nDCG@20 0.4915.
latency_impact: No public runtime change; offline embedding generation and vector scoring completed locally.
indexing_impact: No remote OpenSearch vector index was created in GEN-022; a separate 768-dimensional candidate index is required next.
transferability_result: Deferred; Cranfield offline evidence is strong but remote validation and broader transferability policy remain open.

observed_problem: LTR with lexical and Ollama features did not beat refined PRF; stronger BGE retrieval embeddings needed to be tested as ranking features.
decision: Keep BGE-feature LTR as evaluated offline evidence, but prefer validating simpler BGE hybrid remotely before accepting LTR complexity.
metrics_before: Boosted-tree LTR without BGE reached nDCG@10 0.3216 and binary nDCG@20 0.4457; refined PRF reached nDCG@10 0.3260 and binary nDCG@20 0.4563.
metrics_after: BGE coordinate LTR reached nDCG@10 0.3541 and binary nDCG@20 0.4857; BGE boosted-tree LTR reached nDCG@10 0.3603 and binary nDCG@20 0.4910.
latency_impact: No public runtime change; offline LTR training and evaluation completed locally against cached retrieval pools.
indexing_impact: No OpenSearch LTR plugin, model deployment, or vector index was created in GEN-022.
transferability_result: Deferred; remote vector/hybrid validation should happen before deciding whether LTR complexity is justified.

observed_problem: BGE hybrid retrieval beat refined PRF offline in GEN-022, but the production-shaped OpenSearch kNN path still needed validation before any promotion decision.
decision: Keep remote BGE hybrid as the best production-shaped Cranfield candidate, but do not change the public/default architecture until transferability, latency/cost, and promotion policy are addressed.
metrics_before: Refined PRF reached nDCG@10 0.3260 and binary nDCG@20 0.4563; offline BGE hybrid reached nDCG@10 0.3533 and binary nDCG@20 0.4915.
metrics_after: Remote OpenSearch BGE hybrid reached nDCG@10 0.3533 and binary nDCG@20 0.4926; remote vector-only reached nDCG@10 0.3419 and binary nDCG@20 0.4748.
latency_impact: No public runtime change; remote candidate evaluation completed against a separate OpenSearch index and no public latency claim is made yet.
indexing_impact: Created separate candidate index cranfield-v0-bge-base-en-v15-gen023 with 1,400 Cranfield documents and 768-dimensional BGE vectors; public/default cranfield-v0 was not changed.
transferability_result: Deferred; Cranfield evidence is strong but broader transferability remains required before promotion.

observed_problem: Readers need stable URLs to compare key architecture milestones without changing the public/default latest endpoint or implying candidate promotion.
decision: Add /api/milestones/{milestone}/search and /api/milestones/{milestone}/explain routes for ARCH-0.1 baseline, ARCH-0.2 refined PRF, and ARCH-0.3 BGE; keep BGE runtime disabled with an explicit 501 until query-vector support exists.
metrics_before: Milestone comparison required query parameters or offline artifacts; no stable milestone route family existed.
metrics_after: ARCH-0.1 and ARCH-0.2 PRF milestone routes return live search/explain responses; ARCH-0.3 BGE routes return 501 milestone_runtime_not_enabled with remote validation artifacts and next implementation step.
latency_impact: No public/default runtime change; BGE milestone avoids hidden runtime vector work until explicitly implemented.
indexing_impact: No index changes; remote BGE candidate index from GEN-023 remains separate.
transferability_result: Deferred; endpoint aliases expose evidence but do not promote candidates.

mission: M-0002 Phase 2 - Cross-Domain Validation with BEIR
observed_problem: FEVER (-13.8%) and Climate-FEVER (-12.6%), which share one 5.42M-doc corpus, baseline ~13% below published BM25 numbers while the other 13 datasets reproduce within +/-9% and the aggregate lands on the published ~0.43 average.
decision: Document and proceed. Treat it as a localized OpenSearch-vs-Anserini BM25 config difference on the FEVER-family corpus, not a harness bug; keep the consistent-config baseline for measuring technique deltas rather than re-tuning BM25 per corpus to chase published parity.

mission: M-0002 Phase 2 - Cross-Domain Validation with BEIR
observed_problem: Phase 1 lexical rerankers (coverage, PRF) were shown domain-conditional; the open question was whether the BGE hybrid gain transfers across domains or is also Cranfield-shaped.
decision: Classify BGE hybrid as Universal and admit it to the ARCH-0.5 cross-domain core; keep coverage-rerank and refined PRF as domain-conditional portfolio techniques.
metrics_after: BGE hybrid improves 6/6 datasets +9.5% to +64.0%, zero regressions; vector-only reproduces published BGE-base numbers
transferability_result: Universal across Cranfield+Tier1; Tier2-3 dense eval pending per infra limits
